body.para: This page enumerates the compile-time macro guards defined by the EmulatR V4 root CMakeLists.txt: the CMake option that controls each one, its default state, the build configurations in which it is actually defined, and the code facility it gates. The authoritative source is always the root CMakeLists.txt and the header named in the Source column; this page is a navigation aid, not a substitute.

Each guard follows the same two-stage pattern. A CMake option() declares a cache toggle with a default; an if() block then emits the corresponding -D definition on the Emulatr target. Several guards wrap the definition in a generator-expression predicate so the macro is suppressed in Release even when the option is ON:

The operational consequence: the three diagnostic guards (CHIPSET_DIAG, DIAGNOSTIC_LOGGING, PA_DUMP) are present in Debug and RelWithDebInfo but never in Release, regardless of the option value. To get diagnostics in an optimized build, use RelWithDebInfo, not Release. The two non-predicated guards (USE_OS_PAGES, DEBUG_STEP) are defined in every configuration whenever their option is ON.

Diagnostics-default-off rule (2026-06-10): compiled-in does NOT mean emitting. A diagnostic facility may be present in a Debug/RelWithDebInfo build yet stay SILENT at runtime until the execution explicitly requests it for that run. CSR diagnostics, for example, default muted and are enabled per run via EMULATR_CHIPSET_DIAG_ON (see below). New diagnostics must follow this convention: compile-guarded (absent in Release) AND runtime default-off (opt in per run).

table.style: border-width:0px; border-spacing:0px; border-collapse:collapse; cell-border-width:1px; border-color:#000000; border-style:solid; head-row-background-color:#00ffff; alt-row-background-color:none;

td.para: Wires the CSR_LOG_R / CSR_LOG_W macros so chipset CSR accesses can narrate chip:reg, R/W, value, offset, cpu, cycle. Zero overhead in Release. Compiled in for Debug/RelWithDebInfo but DEFAULT SILENT at runtime (diagnostics-default-off rule): opt in per run via the EMULATR_CHIPSET_DIAG_ON env var, no rebuild.

Selects the OS-backed page allocator (VirtualAlloc on Windows, mmap MAP_ANONYMOUS on POSIX) over the calloc fallback, giving demand-paged commits so the resident set tracks touched memory rather than configured capacity.

Compiles PipelineDriver::step at -O0 (pragma optimize off) so the optimizer does not elide locals during single-step debugging. SLOW: this gates the ~82%-of-runtime hot loop. PERF #0 (2026-06-10) flipped the default to OFF; it was previously always -O0. Turn ON only to debug step().

Compiles in the centralized log facility. Per-subsystem streams (Cbox, Unalign, IntervalTimer, Snapshot, PalRelocation, ChipsetCsr, StepD, Misc) become runtime-toggleable via --log-disable, --log-only, --log-verbose, --log-file. Expands to ((void)0) in Release, so call sites evaluate no arguments. Per the diagnostics-default-off rule, streams are silent until explicitly enabled per run.

Compiles in the physical-address disassembly dump facility: the DUMP_PA and DUMP_DISASM macros plus the AppOptions --dump-disasm flag. Used during bring-up to inspect firmware-decompressed PAL bytes without capturing a full retire trace.

body.para: EMULATR_MEMDIAG is not an option(). It is a hand-edited add_compile_definitions line in the root CMakeLists.txt that is currently commented out (disabled 2026-06-01). When uncommented it emits an fprintf+fflush per retired instruction (PipelineDriver.h MEMDIAG-I, plus MEMDIAG-MTPR), which throttles throughput roughly 900x (down to ~10K cyc/s) in dense PAL TB-fill regions. It was the DS10 TB-fill debug scaffold; re-enable only by uncommenting that line when the diagnostic is needed again.
These are set unconditionally (no option gate) and are listed for completeness; they are not toggles in the usual sense.

table.style: border-width:0px; border-spacing:0px; border-collapse:collapse; cell-border-width:1px; border-color:#000000; border-style:solid; head-row-background-color:#00ffff; alt-row-background-color:none;

td.para: Always on, main executable only (not the test target)
Enables the AXP execution-trace instrumentation on the Emulatr target. Set unconditionally in the main target_compile_definitions block.
Makes every spdlog/fmt symbol inline so no find_package() or link target is required; matches the V1/V3 known-good header-only wiring.
Config-dependent: TRACE in Debug; INFO in Release and RelWithDebInfo
Elides log calls below the active level at the preprocessor, so debug/trace logging is zero cost in optimized builds.

body.para: These share the EMULATR_ prefix but are runtime environment variables or configure-time cache strings, not preprocessor #ifdef guards. They are documented here only to keep the boundary explicit.

table.style: border-width:0px; border-spacing:0px; cell-border-width:0px; border-style:none; head-row-background-color:#00ffff; alt-row-background-color:none;

td.para: Set to 1 to ENABLE CSR diagnostic output for this run; default is silent. Runtime opt-in companion to the EMULATR_CHIPSET_DIAG compile guard, per the diagnostics-default-off rule. (Inverted 2026-06-10 from the former EMULATR_CHIPSET_DIAG_OFF opt-out.)

Overrides the path to the platform device manifest (ds10_platform.json). Default is the CWD-relative file next to the executable. A missing or invalid file is non-fatal: Machine falls back to the compiled-in default DS10 manifest.
Pre-fills the command-line arguments and working directory the Visual Studio / VS Code debugger uses on F5. Configure-time only; not a code guard.

body.para: Override any option at configure time with -D. Examples:

para.text: cmake -DEMULATR_CHIPSET_DIAG=OFF ... # compile out CSR diag entirely
cmake -DEMULATR_DEBUG_STEP=ON ... # compile step() at -O0 (SLOW; debug only)

body.para: CSR diagnostics are compiled in for Debug/RelWithDebInfo but silent by default. To turn them ON for a run on an already-built binary, set the environment variable instead of reconfiguring:
